title: nvm_write fails on first-ever write because nvm_delete returns error for non-existent id
description: nvm_write() unconditionally calls self.nvm_delete(id)? before the add. If no NVM object with that id exists yet (first-time write), nvm_delete calls wh_Client_NvmDestroyObjects which returns WH_ERROR_NOTFOUND (-2104). WolfHsmError::check maps this to Err(WolfHsmError::Wh { code: -2104 }) and the ? operator propagates it — the write fails before the add is ever attempted. Callers must pre-delete or pre-check existence before calling nvm_write, but this is not documented and contradicts the docstring that says 'Write (or overwrite) an NVM object'.
